name: dalfox
summary: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.

documentation.readme: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
usage_sections: Usage: Single URL: dalfox scan http://example.com -b https://callback File Mode: dalfox scan urls.txt --custom-payload mypayloads.txt Pipeline: cat urls.txt | dalfox scan --headers "AuthToken: xxx" Check the CLI reference and Quick start documents for more examples.

arguments.name: --custom-payload
arguments.description: CLI flag '--custom-payload' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --headers
arguments.description: CLI flag '--headers' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --waf-min-confidence
arguments.description: CLI flag '--waf-min-confidence' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: -S
arguments.description: CLI flag '-S' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: brew install dalfox. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `yay -S dalfox`. Further invocations from upstream: `paru -S dalfox`; `nix-shell -p dalfox`. > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with s4l1hs/ShadowStep (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with androguard/androguard (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with MythicAgents/nemesis (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
